description: Fix two additional code quality issues in the browser extension: a leaked setTimeout in probeTabReadiness, and sendTabSyncAll bypassing the per-plugin lock used by all other writers of lastKnownState.

userStories.title: Cancel probeTabReadiness timeout when executeScript settles first
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Promise.race timeout in probeTabReadiness to be cleared when chrome.scripting.executeScript resolves or rejects first, so that no orphaned timers linger after the probe completes.
acceptanceCriteria: The setTimeout timer in probeTabReadiness is cleared (via clearTimeout) when the executeScript promise wins the race
The setTimeout timer is also cleared when executeScript rejects (tab closed, permission error, etc.)
When the timeout wins the race (executeScript is slow), the function still returns false as before
The probeTabReadiness function's external behavior is unchanged — same return values, same timeout duration (IS_READY_TIMEOUT_MS)

userStories.title: Serialize sendTabSyncAll through per-plugin locks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sendTabSyncAll to update the lastKnownState cache through the same per-plugin lock mechanism used by notifyAffectedPlugins and updateLastKnownState, so that concurrent tab events cannot interleave with the sync and produce redundant or stale notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: sendTabSyncAll updates lastKnownState for each plugin through withPluginLock, not via direct map operations
The lastKnownState.clear() call is removed — instead, each plugin's lock callback writes its new state, and plugins that are no longer in the plugin index are cleaned up after the sync
Concurrent checkTabChanged / checkTabRemoved calls for the same plugin are properly serialized against the sync's cache writes (they wait for the sync's lock to release before reading lastKnownState)
The tab.syncAll message to the server and the sp:serverMessage forwards to the side panel still happen after all computations complete (not inside the lock callbacks)
The function's external behavior is unchanged — same payload to the server, same messages to the side panel
